content.problemStatement:
Welcome to the finals of Chef's Combinatorial Games Competition 2019! The finalists Irshad and Mehedi are ready to play against each other no matter what game is given. As it is the final round, Chef prepared a completely new game with the following rules:


There are KKK stacks of stones (numbered 111 through KKK). For each valid iii, the iii-th stack has a threshold HiH_iHi​.
Initially, each stack contains one stone.
The players alternate turns; Irshad plays first.
During each turn, the current player must choose one or two stacks and make a move in each chosen stack.
To make a move in a stack iii containing xxx stones at the beginning of the current turn, the current player must add one or more stones to this stack in such a way that the resulting number of stones yyy (y>xy \gt xy>x) divides that stack's threshold HiH_iHi​. (If this stack already contains HiH_iHi​ stones, it is impossible to make a move.)
When it is impossible to make a move in any stack, the current player loses the game.
Of course, Irshad and Mehedi are brilliant programmers, so they can easily play this game optimally for given thresholds. Therefore, to spice up the game, Chef chose KKK people from the audience and gave one stack to each of them. He also provided a set S={x1,x2,…,xN}S = \{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_N\}S={x1​,x2​,…,xN​}. Each of the chosen people should uniformly randomly, independently from all other people, choose the threshold of his/her stack from the set SSS.

While this is happening, Irshad asks you: "Hey, in how many ways can these people set the thresholds such that I win?" You need to find the answer modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9+7109+7).
constraints: x1,x2,…,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1​,x2​,…,xN​ are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a single integer xix_ixi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of ways to choose the thresholds such that Irshad wins, modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9+7109+7).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are two choices of thresholds that are losing for Irshad: (2,2,2)(2, 2, 2)(2,2,2) and (4,4,4)(4, 4, 4)(4,4,4). If these thresholds are used, Mehedi always wins regarless of Irshad's moves. All six remaining choices are winning for Irshad.
